title: Unexpected assertion behaviour
body:
Dear Verilator team,

I have run into an issue that I am unsure how to resolve.
I have a design containing signal stability assertions, of the form:

`assert property (@(posedge clk) disable iff (~reset_n) ~rval | (raddr0_r==addr0_i));`

Sometimes, with minor changes to the design not affecting the signals in the property, the assertions will start to fail exactly when reset is deasserted, when Verilator is launched with `--assert` enabled on compilation and runtime.
Additionally, when compiled with additional trace options `--trace-fst --trace-structs` , the assertions do not fail. I am able to make them fail again by recompiling without the trace options.

Any guidance would be appreciated.
Thank you,
Best regards,
Tomasz
